_meta.description: Machine-executable decision tree. Given company inputs, routes to one of four cover pathways and one of six content slide types per slide. No subjective judgment calls — all branches are deterministic.

cover_pathway_tree.description: Four-question tree. First YES wins. Evaluated top-to-bottom against companyName and brandContext.

concept_domains.question: Does the company name carry a myth, legend, historical figure, folklore character, or cultural archetype?
exclusion_rules: surname without mythology (e.g. McDonald, Ferrari, Ford)
invented portmanteau (e.g. Spotify, Kodak, Xerox, Google)
pure descriptive compound (e.g. Facebook, YouTube, Airbnb)

illustration_styles.medieval-european: 19th century steel engraving, oval vignette frame, crosshatch linework
illustration_styles.ancient-greek-roman: classical line engraving, marble relief style

visual_elements.frame_decorative_detail: foliage, architectural, or heraldic elements at base appropriate to era
visual_elements.subject_rule: Most unexpected but precise rendering — animal substitution, role reversal, or anachronistic precision. Wit layer is required.
visual_elements.illustration_tint_rule: Same hue family as background, 1-2 tones darker. Never black.

concept_domains.selection_bias: Prefer most unexpected interpretation over literal. Fox archer beats human Robin Hood. Warrior queen beats generic Amazon logo.

concept_domains.question: Does the company have a single universally recognized human moment inside its core product interaction — one every user has had and could describe in a sentence?
exclusion_rules: brand whose product is a physical object, not a service
brand whose experience is entirely internal/solitary with no interpersonal moment
brand with no single universally shared moment (too varied per user)

visual_elements.camera_position: inside the brand's core interaction environment, looking into the experience
visual_elements.human_presence: silhouette or shape only — never face, never identifiable
visual_elements.motion_treatment: long-exposure-blur if brand involves movement, otherwise sharp
visual_elements.color_source: practical light sources within photograph only — never designed color elements

quote_rules.test: read aloud — if it sounds like ad copy, rewrite it
quote_rules.position: upper area of canvas, slight left bias, ~24-30% from top

concept_domains.selection_bias: The most awkward, intimate, or universally embarrassing moment beats the aspirational moment.

concept_domains.question: Does the company's logo or primary emblem depict a real-world subject (animal, natural object, figure) that is MORE iconic than the product itself?
exclusion_rules: abstract or geometric logo (Nike swoosh, Adidas stripes, Google G)
human face or portrait logo (likeness rights)
logo subject cannot be photographed or rendered with emotional impact

visual_elements.pose_rule: Must correspond precisely to logo pose. Viewer must simultaneously recognize the logo AND see the living creature. Pose correspondence is the concept.
visual_elements.subject_color: warm near-black (#0A0505 range) — not neutral black
visual_elements.detail_level: near-silhouette — shape readable, surface detail minimal
visual_elements.lighting: single hard key light, upper-left or upper-right, no fill
visual_elements.lighting_effect: faint edge highlight on one surface only, all else falls to near-black
visual_elements.composition: subject center-right, open color field on left side
visual_elements.ground: faint hue-matched shadow beneath subject — no visible ground plane

concept_domains.selection_bias: The gap between the flat logo and the living creature IS the concept. Closer pose correspondence = stronger emotional charge.

concept_domains.question: Does the company have an iconic product object immediately recognizable without packaging or brand context?
exclusion_rules: brand whose product is not visually iconic or requires context to identify
brand already in luxury category (elevation has no gap to exploit)
brand whose logo symbol is more iconic than any product

visual_elements.studio_setup: black studio, no environmental context, no packaging, no branding
visual_elements.lighting: single hard key light, no fill, upper-right or upper-left

composition_devices.cascade: multiple instances floating in vertical diagonal, scale progression (nearest largest), rotation variation 3-8 degrees alternating
composition_devices.single-specimen: one instance, centered or offset to golden ratio, maximum negative space
composition_devices.explosion: product deconstructed, components floating apart from center

visual_elements.shadow_pool: faint hue-matched shadow beneath lowest instance — same hue as background, 20-30% darker
visual_elements.post_processing: crush blacks, push highlights, warm or cool grade matching product material

concept_domains.selection_bias: The wider the gap between how the brand normally shows this product and how this shot renders it, the stronger the concept. Fast food lit like jewellery beats luxury goods lit like luxury goods.

color_system.description: Pre-determined color derivation. Pure function: f(brandHex, pathway, energy) → colorSystem. See dispatch-phase1-color-derivation.md for full algorithm.

state_modifiers.description: Dispatch does not use sentiment-based state modifiers like Dark Museum (broken/rusted/pristine). Color derivation and pathway selection encode state implicitly.

content_slide_routing.description: Determines slide type for each content slide. Applied after cover. Uses declared type if provided, otherwise infers from content.

inference_conditions: default — used when no other type matches

slide_archetypes.layout_rule: full-bleed. Visual statement owns entire canvas. Bottom 15% clear or dark for footer zone.
special_rules: pathway determined by cover_pathway_tree
no text in generated image
no logos in generated image — coordinates output only
footer zone outputs placeholder coordinates, no generated content

slide_archetypes.layout_rule: TONE_A background. Cormorant Garamond italic 64-84px centered vertically. Context strip active.
special_rules: serif headline — the only T4 slide with Cormorant Garamond
marks structural chapter transitions in carousel
accent line always present at zone2-zone4 boundary

slide_archetypes.layout_rule: TONE_B background. Primary number DM Sans 800 120-140px. Secondary number 48px accentHex. Internal divider 1px accentHex 60% width.
special_rules: maximum 2 numbers per slide — 3+ stats = split across slides
tabular figures if font supports it
accent color on secondary number only — never primary

slide_archetypes.layout_rule: TONE_A background. Opening quote mark 96px accentHex. Quote body Cormorant Garamond italic 32-44px. Attribution DM Sans 500 15px.
special_rules: opening quotation mark is oversized and in accentHex — the only typographic moment of color
attribution indented 16px under quote body
quote length scales font size: 1-2 lines → 44px | 3 lines → 38px | 4 lines → 32px | max 4 lines

slide_archetypes.layout_rule: TONE_B background. Optional headline DM Sans 700 28px. Body copy DM Sans 400 18-20px textSecondary. Max 4 sentences.
special_rules: left-aligned body copy — never justified
headline is optional — omit if context strip is self-explanatory
no serif on body slides

slide_archetypes.layout_rule: TONE_B background. List title DM Sans 600 22px. Items with accentHex 4px square markers. Max 5 items.
special_rules: markers are accent color filled squares — the one typographic accent on list slides
sequential markers (01 02 03) replace squares when sequence matters
max 5 items — split into 2 list slides if more needed
item spacing 16px between items

slide_archetypes.layout_rule: TONE_A background. Closing statement Cormorant Garamond italic 52-64px centered. Optional action line DM Sans 400 16px accentHex centered.
special_rules: ONLY centered layout in the entire system — signals closure
no other slide type uses center alignment
context strip reads 'DISPATCH · END'
action line in accentHex — subtle CTA, not aggressive

slide_count_rules.single_card_mode: slideCount === 1 produces cover only (backward compatibility with Quartr Cards format)
slide_count_rules.enforcement: pipeline rejects input.slides.length > 10 with error message

multi_model_routing.description: Model preference per pathway. See dispatch-image-system.md for full prompt templates.

name-archaeology.reason: Best at era-specific illustration and crosshatch linework

experience-capture.reason: Best at following complex scene composition and atmosphere

product-elevation.reason: Best at studio product photography and dark studio lighting

symbol-literalization.reason: Best at chiaroscuro lighting and near-silhouette animal subjects
